title: for Loops
description: Iterate over sequences and vectors using for loops.

slides.title: The for Loop 🔁
slides.content:
A `for` loop repeats code for each element in a vector or sequence.

Syntax:
```r
for (variable in sequence) {
  # code to repeat
}
```

On each iteration, `variable` takes the next value from `sequence`.

quizData.explanation: It prints each element on a separate line with the index.

slides.title: Looping Over Vectors
slides.content:
You can loop over any vector, not just numeric sequences.

```r
fruits <- c("apple", "banana", "cherry")
for (fruit in fruits) {
  print(paste("I like", fruit))
}
```

slides.title: Loop Over Vector
Accumulating Results
slides.content:
Often you want to store results from each iteration. Initialize an empty vector and fill it.

```r
results <- c()  # empty vector
for (i in 1:10) {
  results[i] <- i^2
}
```

slides.title: Accumulate Results
Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content: Pre-allocating vectors (like `numeric(10)`) is much faster than growing them inside a loop.
